news.headline: Bird flu outbreak stability uncertain; experts unsure if decline in cases is permanent.

news.summary: In a recent report, it was highlighted that a record number of 3.5 million job positions were created in the last quarter, exceeding all expectations. The unemployment rate dropped to 4.2%, the lowest it has been in over a decade. The economy grew by 6.5% in the last year, demonstrating a strong recovery from the pandemic's impact. Additionally, wages increased by an average of 3.8% across various sectors, indicating an improvement in overall economic conditions. These positive trends have been attributed to government stimulus packages and successful vaccination efforts driving consumer confidence and spending. The outlook for the upcoming quarters remains optimistic as the country continues on its path to economic stability.
news.headline: Arm issues cautious forecast, adding to chipmakers' concerns.

news.summary: Browser mining has seen a resurgence in 2025 with tools like CryptoTab Browser, Pi Network, and YouHolder after the shutdown of Coinhive in 2019. Monero remains popular for browser mining, while CryptoTab focuses on Bitcoin. Browser mining is not very profitable in 2025, mainly serving as a novelty or experimentation rather than a serious income source. Leaving a mid-range laptop mining all day could cost over $10 per month in electricity for a minimal return in mined crypto. Browser mining struggles to compete with GPU or ASIC setups in terms of efficiency and profitability. Though it consumes less power than ASIC farming, the cumulative power draw from inefficient personal devices can be significant. Browser mining is evolving with WebAssembly, improving script efficiency for a smoother user experience. The audience for browser mining includes casual users, newcomers to crypto, and the crypto-curious. While it may not lead to significant earnings, browser mining lowers the barrier to entry, especially for those without advanced hardware or in lower-income regions. Future developments in browser mining could include integrating decentralized finance and improving market and regulation compliance. Alternative use cases like Brave browser's Basic Attention Tokens and connecting browser mining with yield farming in the DeFi world offer additional passive earning opportunities. Overall, browser mining in 2025 is not a lucrative endeavor but provides a gateway for newcomers to explore the world of crypto.
news.headline: Browser mining in 2025: CryptoTab Browser, Pi Network, and YouHolder offer passive earnings but not profitable due to electricity costs exceeding crypto value. Mining limits and questions arise.

news.summary: Coinbase, the largest US cryptocurrency exchange, is acquiring Deribit, a major crypto derivatives trading platform, for $2.9 billion. The deal includes $700 million in cash and 11 million shares of Coinbase stock. This move aims to help Coinbase enter the profitable crypto derivatives market and enhance its global presence. The acquisition is pending regulatory approvals and is expected to close by year-end. Reports suggest the deal values Deribit between $4 billion and $5 billion. This strategic deal could potentially allow Coinbase to accelerate its international growth strategy. Further updates on this developing story will be provided as new information emerges.
news.headline: Coinbase set to acquire Deribit for $2.9B, expanding into crypto derivatives market. Deal includes $700M cash and 11M shares. Regulatory approvals pending. Development story.

news.summary: SS Innovations is preparing to submit a De Novo application to the FDA for their SSi Mantra 3 Surgical Robotic System, intended for various medical purposes. This advanced robotic system aims to enhance surgical procedures and improve patient outcomes. The submission process involves detailed documentation and evaluation, with potential approval enabling widespread use of the technology in the healthcare sector. The system's innovative features set it apart in the market, with its potential to revolutionize surgical practices. The application signifies a significant milestone for SS Innovations in advancing healthcare technology.
news.headline: SS Innovations to seek FDA approval for SSi Mantra 3 Surgical Robotic System for various indications.

news.summary: In New York, a non-profit initiative named Brandywine Cares, which was initiated in 2020 by Lafayette Real Estate, a prominent single-family rental real estate asset manager, has strengthened its dedication over the last 18 months. The program aims to support tenants living in Lafayette's rental homes and beyond, empowering individuals in need and enhancing their communities. The initiative has shown increasing success in its efforts.
news.headline: Brandywine Cares, non-profit by Lafayette Real Estate, deepens impact in local communities, aiding tenants and others.

news.summary: In New York, VSS Capital Partners, a private investment firm specializing in the lower middle market, has shared that its portfolio company, The HFW Companies, has successfully acquired its 10th strategic growth partner for its platform. The recent addition, Southwest Engineers, based in Texas, marks a significant milestone for HFW with VSS backing. The financial details of the transaction have not been revealed. This acquisition reflects VSS's continued commitment to collaborating with founders and management teams to foster company growth, a strategy they have been executing for over three decades.
news.headline: VSS Capital Partners' company HFW adds 10th partner firm; acquired Southwest Engineers. Financial terms not disclosed.

news.summary: Carvana Co (NYSE: CVNA) posted strong first-quarter financial results, surpassing expectations. The company reported $4.23 billion in revenue, exceeding the estimated $3.98 billion. Their first-quarter earnings were $1.51 per share, well above the anticipated 60 cents per share. Carvana's performance in Q1 saw a record-breaking number of retail units sold, along with achieving peak profitability and the highest customer net promoter score in almost three years. Ernie Garcia, co-founder of Carvana, expressed satisfaction with the results. Details can be found on the Benzinga website.
news.headline: Carvana's Q1 results exceed expectations with revenue of $4.23 billion, earnings of $1.51 per share. Analysts increase forecasts.

news.summary: The balance sheet has been strengthened through $30 million in debt-to-equity conversions, enhancing support for strategic growth and increasing stockholder value.
news.headline: ScanTech AI eliminates $30M debt with equity restricted shares, boosting balance sheet and protecting stockholder value.

news.summary: The first phase of due diligence has been completed in a timely manner as planned. The second phase of due diligence is set to be finished by June 2, 2025.
news.headline: VivoPower progresses non-binding takeover worth US$180M, completes Phase I due diligence on time. Phase II due by June 2, 2025.

The guide highlights the tool's capabilities and its impact in the industry. Stonebranch's Universal Automation Center has garnered attention for its innovative approach and features, positioning it as a key player in the market. news.summary: OMNY Health, a prominent healthcare ecosystem focusing on compliant real-world data insights at scale, revealed today the broadening of its data network with the incorporation of over 300 clinical assessment measures. These disease-tailored measures encompass data stemming from clinical severity markers, surveys, and questionnaires, exemplified by the Crohn’s Disease Activity Index (CDAI), the Harvey Bradshaw index for Gastroenterology, as well as the Asthma Control Test and COPD Assessment.

news.summary: In Frankfurt, Germany, a recent report revealed that automakers in the United States have ramped up their investment in automation. The total number of industrial robots installed in the car industry saw a significant increase of 10.7% in 2024, totaling at 13,700 units. These preliminary findings were shared by the International Federation of Robotics (IFR). The report highlighted that the United States boasts one of the most automated car industries globally, with the ratio of robots to factory workers ranking fifth when compared to countries like Japan, Germany, and China. Takayuki.
news.headline: US car industry sees 10.7% rise in robots installed, hitting 13,700 units in 2024. US ranks 5th in robot to worker ratio globally.

news.summary: Shiba Inu (SHIB) has experienced a significant increase in its burn rate, coupled with a 5% rise in price, leading traders to anticipate a potential bull run. The latest cryptocurrency prices show Dogecoin (DOGE) at $0.1841 with a market cap of $27.5 billion, up by 6.7% in the last 24 hours and 2.8% over the past week. Shiba Inu is priced at $0.00001348, boasting a market cap of $7.9 billion, marking a 5.3% increase in the last day but a slight dip of 1.3% in the last seven days. Pepe is currently valued at $0.059292, holding a market cap of $3.9 billion, showing a notable surge of 14.4% in the last 24 hours and 2.9% over the past week. Noteworthy trader notes include Heber Mayen, a long-term SHIB investor, expressing optimism by stating that Shiba Inu has likely overcome its recent challenges and anticipates a potential breakout soon. For the full story, visit Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Shiba Inu's burn rate rises by 4,833% with a 5% price surge, hinting at possible Golden Bull Run. Dogecoin hits $27.5B market cap, Shiba Inu at $7.9B.

news.summary: International Flavors & Fragrances Inc. (NYSE: IFF) surpassed expectations with their first-quarter earnings report. CEO Erik Fyrwald attributed this success to strong execution and growth in most business sectors. This performance, along with efficiency measures, led to a notable rise in profitability for IFF. The complete article can be accessed on Benzinga.com.

The project is scheduled to commence its first delivery in 2026. Notably, Rio AI City will be powered entirely by renewable and certified energy sources. Moreover, the city will operate waterless systems as part of its sustainable infrastructure.
news.headline: Elea Announces Rio AI City: Brazilian data center with 3.2 GW capacity, starting with 1.5 GW in 2026, 100% renewable energy.

news.summary: A long-term agreement commemorates the common values of skill, determination, and enthusiasm between whiskey and baseball, paying tribute to the enduring essence of these two beloved American customs. The partnership was announced in FRANKFORT, Ky., on May 8, 2025. Traveller Blended Whiskey, a collaborative effort between Buffalo Trace Distillery's Master Distiller and others, will be at the center of this alliance.

This method bypasses conventional lending procedures and instead provides business owners with funds through their existing software solutions. This innovative strategy aims to streamline the process for entrepreneurs.

news.summary: In Boston, Hunt Capital Partners has transferred its ownership stake as a limited partner in two affordable housing communities to local nonprofits. This move signifies a shift in ownership for these properties to organizations focused on community welfare. The transfer includes a significant financial component, with an estimated value of [insert number] being shifted towards the nonprofits. This decision contributes to the ongoing efforts to maintain and support affordable housing initiatives in the region, reflecting a broader trend of corporate entities redirecting resources to local community organizations.
news.headline: Hunt Capital Partners transfers ownership of two Boston affordable housing developments to local nonprofits.

news.summary: Saint Louis is gearing up for the prestigious Cairns Cup, a high-level female chess competition organized by the Saint Louis Chess Club. Running from June 10 to June 20, the event boasts an increased prize pool of $250,000. This tournament, named after Dr. Jeanne Cairns Sinquefield, one of the club's co-founders, signifies the institution's dedication to promoting female participation in chess. Dr. Jeanne Cairns Sinquefield expressed delight in hosting renowned female chess talents, emphasizing the club's consistent efforts to support women in the chess world.
news.headline: Top female chess players to compete in 2025 Cairns Cup in Saint Louis with $250,000 prize fund; tournament honors Dr. Jeanne Cairns Sinquefield.

news.summary: A roundtable discussion hosted by the Association for Healthcare Denial and Appeal Management (AHDAM) is scheduled for May 21st at 2:00 PM EST in Towson, Maryland. news.summary: David Stein, who has been with Associated Banc-Corp (NYSE: ASB) for over 20 years, will retire by the end of 2025. He will transition to the roles of executive vice president, strategic advisor, and Madison market president. This announcement was made on May 8, 2025, and Stein will continue in these positions until his retirement.
news.headline: David Stein retiring after 20+ years at Associated Bank, to become EVP & strategic advisor by the end of 2025.

news.summary: In Las Vegas, Wired Real Estate Group (WiredRE), a data center development firm specializing in AI, revealed the completion of a major project in Dallas. This project involved the development and sale of a massive AI infrastructure site exceeding 1,000 megawatts. Considered one of the largest AI-centered data center projects in 2024, the site was acquired by a leading global cloud service provider. Within a remarkable timeframe of under 12 months, WiredRE turned this site around from financial distress to a fully operational and power-equipped asset.
news.headline: WiredRE sells 1,000+ megawatt AI infrastructure site in Dallas, among largest AI data center projects in 2024.

news.summary: According to a recent survey by Transaction Network Services (TNS), the increase in scams and the ongoing problem of high volumes of robocalls are leading consumers to request businesses to brand their calls. The survey revealed that 76% of Americans would rather interact with companies that use branded calling compared to those that do not. This preference provides a significant edge for organizations aiming to improve customer experience through the voice channel.
news.headline: 76% consumers prefer businesses that brand calls to combat scams, TNS survey finds. Competitive advantage for CX optimization.

news.summary: Costa Mesa, California - A recent survey conducted by MeridianLink, Inc. revealed that around 30% of homeowners are contemplating acquiring a home equity loan or a HELOC (Home Equity Line of Credit) within the upcoming year. MeridianLink, Inc. (NYSE: MLNK) conducted the survey, which highlights the growing interest in leveraging home equity, among homeowners.
news.headline: Nearly 30% of Homeowners Considering Home Equity Loans Amid High Interest Rates and Economic Uncertainty

news.summary: AmniSphere in Houston is changing the game in AI integration by providing rapid, scalable, and people-focused computer vision solutions for companies.
news.headline: Amniscient launches AmniSphere, a breakthrough in computer vision AI, providing fast, scalable solutions for businesses.

news.summary: In Columbus, Ohio, Pharmavite LLC, a leading nutrition company known for Nature Made vitamins, is marking the debut of its cutting-edge manufacturing and research & development center in New Albany. The state-of-the-art facility spans 225,000 square feet and represents a significant $250 million commitment, signaling Pharmavite's inaugural presence in the area. The venture is poised to generate 225 employment opportunities, with prospects for additional growth at the site. Over its 50-year legacy, Pharmavite has solidified its presence in the industry.
news.headline: Pharmavite opens $250 million production facility in New Albany, Ohio, creating 225 jobs and 225,000 sq ft.

news.summary: Levi Strauss & Co. introduced the "These City Walls" project in San Francisco, which aims to commission 25 new murals in celebration of the 25th anniversary of the company's Community Day. The initiative is a part of LS&Co.'s commitment to aid in the city's revitalization by providing grants to local groups, engaging employees in volunteer work, and collaborating with similar businesses and non-profit organizations. This undertaking marks the beginning of the mural project, symbolizing the company's dedication to supporting the community.
news.headline: Levi Strauss & Co. to fund 25 new murals in San Francisco for Community Day, supporting city revitalization efforts.

news.summary: In Minneapolis, Pillsbury has launched a new line of refrigerated cookie dough called Pillsbury BIG COOKIES, which offers bakery-style cookies for everyday enjoyment. The cookies are over three times larger than Pillsbury's classic size and are designed to be baked at home in 17-21 minutes. The BIG COOKIES line includes three indulgent flavors, providing a satisfying treat for cookie lovers. This new offering aims to make cookie day a regular occurrence, eliminating the need to wait for weekends or special occasions to enjoy fresh, delicious cookies.
news.headline: Pillsbury introduces BIG COOKIES cookie dough - 3x larger, bakes in 17-21 minutes, 3 indulgent flavors.

news.summary: In a recent announcement, it was revealed that the premier commercial real estate event, ICSC LAS VEGAS, is scheduled to take place in Las Vegas, Nevada from May 18-20, 2025. The event, known as the world’s largest of its kind, will bring together top professionals and organizations from the industry. Attendees can anticipate a range of networking, dealmaking, and professional development opportunities during the event. It aims to showcase how the commercial real estate sector remains a key driver of growth across various economic conditions.
news.headline: ICSC LAS VEGAS 2025 returns May 18-20, largest real estate gathering in Las Vegas with industry professionals.

news.summary: In Quincy, Massachusetts, J.Jill, Inc. (NYSE: JILL) revealed the recipients of its 23rd annual J.Jill Compassion Fund grants. The fund aims to assist women in advancing their lives and that of their families by aiding community organizations. Specifically, the fund supports disadvantaged women in attaining self-sufficiency. This year's grants mark another step in the company's commitment to empowering women.
news.headline: J.Jill awards grants to support disadvantaged women in achieving self-sufficiency through 23rd annual Compassion Fund.

news.summary: Dogwood Therapeutics, Inc. has started dosing patients in the Halneuron ® Chemotherapy Induced Neuropathic Pain Phase 2b study, with interim data expected in Q4 2025. The company has $17.5M in cash, ensuring operational funding through Q1 2026. They aim to become the first FDA-approved therapy for CINP. Halneuron ® has proven effective in treating general cancer pain and CINP, potentially expanding to diabetic neuropathy and acute surgical pain. The company's research pipeline includes IMC-2 for Long-COVID and IMC-1 for Fibromyalgia. Financially, R&D expenses were $2.4M, G&A expenses were $2M, and net loss for Q1 2025 was $12.2M. Nasdaq compliance has been met. Dogwood Therapeutics focuses on non-opioid analgesics and antiviral treatments, particularly targeting the Na V 1.7 sodium channel for pain management. Their innovative therapies offer promising solutions for pain and fatigue-related disorders.
news.headline: Dogwood Therapeutics reports Q1 2025 financial results:- Cash on hand: $17.5M till Q1 2026- Commenced dosing in Phase 2b CINP study- Interim data expected in Q4 2025- Stockholders' equity regained to $2.5M- $4.8M capital raised- $12.2M net loss in Q1 2025- Halneuron for CINP & other pain conditions- IMC-1 for FM, IMC-2 for Long-COVID - Research efforts in non-opioid analgesic & antiviral programs.

(Nasdaq: RICK) is set to release its financial results for the fiscal 2025 second quarter that ended on March 31, 2025, after the market closes on Monday, May 12. news.summary: In Long Branch, New Jersey, Raw Generation, a prominent cold-pressed juice and nutrition company, has revealed a new product line called Little Sippers. These nutritionist-formulated cold-pressed juices are specifically tailored for children. Each set, designed for weekly consumption, contains juices comprising 40 raw fruits and vegetables, providing a diverse range of delicious and healthy options for kids. This offering enables parents to seamlessly integrate vital nutrients into their children's diets without any additional sugars or artificial additives. This initiative is particularly significant in the U.S., where around two million children face nutritional deficiencies.
news.headline: Raw Generation releases Little Sippers, kid-friendly juices with 40 fruits and veggies per set, no sugar/artificial ingredients.

news.summary: Skyworks Solutions Inc (NASDAQ: SWKS) saw a premarket decline in shares after announcing their second quarter FY25 results post-market on Wednesday. The company exceeded revenue expectations with $953.2 million compared to the consensus of $951.47 million. Additionally, their adjusted EPS of $1.24 surpassed estimates of $1.20. CEO Phil Brace highlighted the return of over $600 million to shareholders through share buybacks and dividends, marking the highest quarterly return in company history. Despite market fluctuations, Brace emphasized the company's unwavering focus...Complete details can be accessed on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Skyworks reports $953.2M revenue, $1.24 EPS in Q2, highest quarterly return of $600M despite stock decline.

news.summary: Groupon Inc. (NASDAQ: GRPN) saw an uptick in premarket trading on Thursday following the release of its first-quarter financial results. The company exceeded revenue expectations, reporting $117.2 million compared to estimates of $115.5 million. Despite a 5% decrease in revenue year-over-year (with a 4% increase on a FX-neutral basis), Groupon posted an EPS of 18 cents, beating projections of an 11-cent loss. Gross billings for the quarter amounted to $386.5 million, marking a 1% increase year-over-year and a 2% rise on a FX-neutral basis. It is worth noting that over 5 million Americans face potential health coverage loss by 2034 due to proposed GOP Medicaid cuts, according to the CBO. For more details, visit the full story on Benzinga.com.
news.headline: Groupon surprises with Q1 profit, beats revenue estimates at $117.2 million, EPS at 18 cents, and gross billings at $386.5 million. Profit outlook raised.

news.summary: Boston-based tech company Sonde has teamed up with Saaya to offer voice-based mental health tracking and support services to blue-collar workers in industries such as energy, manufacturing, and logistics. This partnership aims to address the mental health needs of these workers by providing accessible and user-friendly tools.
news.headline: Sonde Health and Saaya Health partner to provide voice-based mental health tracking to blue-collar workers in energy, manufacturing, and logistics industries.

news.summary: A report on the State of Strategic Response Management in 2025 was recently published by Responsive in collaboration with the Association of Proposal Management Professionals (APMP) in Beaverton, Oregon. This report provides valuable insights and trends in the strategic response management landscape for the year 2025.
